game_name: Battletoads & Double Dragon: The Ultimate Team

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic for all beat 'em up sections
Introduces edge-hanging and finger-vulnerability to enemy stepping
Expands movement vocabulary beyond ground-based combat
Introduces backward-kick mechanics and momentum-based movement
New movement mode requires distinct combat retraining
Fundamental game-mode change from beat 'em up to shooter; handles asteroids, mines, UFOs
Pure skill-based pattern recognition after 5 levels of mixed mechanic introduction

typical_tension_triggers: Rope section with multiple enemies approaching
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating challenge with major tension spike at Level 4 genre shift; two-player shared life pool intensifies pressure in cooperative mode

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduces beat 'em up fundamentals with rope mechanics; enemies attack in manageable waves
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Speeder bike and walker leg sections demand new motor controls; falling-finger instant-fail adds precision stakes
difficulty_curve.late_game: Genre transition to shooter (Level 4) followed by missile hull boarding, then pure boss gauntlet (Levels 6-7)
notable_spikes: Level 4: beat 'em up → shooter paradigm shift with projectile dodging
Level 6-7: Multi-phase boss encounters require pattern memorization

notable_level_patterns.description: Level 1 establishes beat 'em up baseline with rope swinging, introduces hanging-edge finger mechanic vulnerability
Level 2 expands spatial navigation within confined ship corridors; mixed enemy encounters with hanging sections
Levels 3-4 introduce speeder bikes and walker legs as alternative movement modes with distinct attack vocabularies (backward kicks, leg bashes)

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Genre Mode Shift: Beat 'em Up to Shooter
notable_level_patterns.description: Level 4 fundamentally changes gameplay from close-combat to ranged avoidance; player controls small spacecraft dodging asteroids, mines, UFOs from Dark Queen
Level 5 bridges shooter and final boss gauntlet; goal to board escape vessel
Levels 6-7 are pure skill tests: Shadow Boss single encounter, then Dark Queen final multi-phase fight; no wave enemies, pattern-based only
